description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, featuring prominent weathering and rust marks that give it a rugged, urban appearance. The hydrant has two side outlets and one front-facing outlet, each capped and secured with metal chains to prevent loss of the caps. The top of the hydrant is fitted with an operating nut, used for opening the water valve, and the body is reinforced with thick metal bands and flanges. The surface is predominantly painted red, but the paint is chipped and worn, revealing patches of bare metal and rust, especially around the edges and joints. The chains and caps are metallic, showing signs of oxidation and use. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three capped outlets, metal chains, and visible rust and wear, typical of urban streetscapes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet with a cap and chain, the domed top, and both side outlets partially visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the right side outlet and its chain, with the front outlet and top nut also visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, both side outlets, and the chains hanging down, with no outlets facing directly forward.
description_view_3: Angled view from the left, showing the left side outlet and chain, as well as the front outlet and top nut.
